completed_tasks.name: dBN framework: 4 paths closed (collision, log-concavity, energy, Euler product)
Jensen polynomials: Turan verified, sigma^2~1.37, GORZ connection
Jacobi matrix: prime signature in b_k, Stieltjes property verified (1800 pts)
M-function: RH as single inequality, verified 4000 pts, decomposition into Gamma vs zeta
M-positivity for large t: PROVED (Gamma dominance, unconditional)
First-order identity: g'(t) = sum gamma_n/(t-gamma_n)^2 with factorization
Quantitative dominance: fails by 110x (same Levinson gap)
Grok 5-avenue assessment: scored 1-7/10, identified Li criterion as best next target

remaining_tasks.name: SESSION 17: Li's criterion attack — compute lambda_n from Euler product side
Express lambda_n via explicit formula as prime sums, check positivity
Connect lambda_n to M-function decomposition (same positivity in different basis?)
Avenue 4: test M-function with truncated Euler product (finite sum)
Avenue 5: engage with Connes prolate operator (2024-2025 papers)

decisions.decision: Li's criterion is the priority for Session 17
decisions.rationale: Grok identifies it as cleanest identity-to-proof route. lambda_n have explicit formula representations as prime sums. Connects to our M-function decomposition. Scored 3/10 in session 16 — most underexplored high-potential avenue.

decisions.decision: The M-function M(s) = Im[(s-1/2)*conj(xi'/xi)] > 0 is equivalent to RH
decisions.rationale: Proved via Stieltjes property of squared-zero transform. Non-circular (computable from mpmath). Partial unconditional results: sigma>1 proved, large t proved.

decisions.decision: Quantitative gap for pointwise M-positivity is 110x
decisions.rationale: Off-line zero spike ~gamma/alpha^2 vs on-line background ~p*gamma*pi^2/(3d^2). Need c>5.48, have c~0.05. Same structural gap as Levinson (pointwise vs average).

next_action: Start Session 17 with Li's criterion: (1) Compute lambda_n directly, (2) Express via explicit formula as prime sums, (3) Check if M-function Gamma-vs-zeta decomposition maps to lambda_n positivity in a useful way. The lambda_n are defined as sum_rho [1-(1-1/rho)^n] and RH iff lambda_n > 0 for all n >= 1.
context_notes: Session 16 was the longest single session: 20+ commits, 3 preprints, explored dBN (closed), Jacobi matrix (new), Stieltjes/M-function (new formulation of RH), first-order identity with factorization, quantitative dominance bound (110x gap). Scored against Grok's 5 avenues: best on Avenue 3 (spectral, 7/10), worst on Avenue 4 (truncated Euler, 1/10). Li's criterion (Avenue 2, scored 3/10) identified as most promising for next session. Key scripts: dbn_explorer.py, dbn_analytical.py, dbn_logconcavity.py, dbn_alpha_derivation.py, dbn_lambda_bound.py, dbn_jensen.py, dbn_explicit_formula.py, jacobi_zeta.py, jacobi_zeta_v2.py, jacobi_from_primes.py, jacobi_schur.py, jacobi_stieltjes.py, jacobi_M_function.py. Environment: /c/Python313/python.exe.
